title: Zendesk Ticket
description: A Zendesk Support ticket representing a customer service request. Tickets are the core entity in Zendesk Support, capturing the communication between a customer and support agents. Each ticket has a lifecycle from creation through resolution, with statuses, priorities, assignees, and a full audit trail of changes and comments.

id.description: Automatically assigned unique identifier for the ticket.

url.description: The API URL of the ticket resource.

external_id.description: An optional ID from an external system used to link the ticket to records outside Zendesk.

type.description: The classification of the ticket. 'problem' tickets can have linked 'incident' tickets. 'task' tickets support due dates.

subject.description: The subject line of the ticket, typically a brief summary of the issue.

raw_subject.description: The original subject as entered by the requester, before any processing by Zendesk.

description.description: The first comment on the ticket, set at creation time. Read-only after the ticket is created.

priority.description: The urgency level of the ticket.

status.description: The current state of the ticket in its lifecycle.

recipient.description: The original recipient email address of the ticket.

requester_id.description: The ID of the user who requested the ticket (the customer).

submitter_id.description: The ID of the user who submitted the ticket. Can differ from the requester when an agent creates a ticket on behalf of a customer.

assignee_id.description: The ID of the agent currently assigned to the ticket.

organization_id.description: The ID of the organization associated with the ticket.

group_id.description: The ID of the agent group assigned to the ticket.

collaborator_ids.description: IDs of users currently CC'd on the ticket.

follower_ids.description: IDs of agents currently following the ticket for internal notifications.

email_cc_ids.description: IDs of agents or end users currently CC'd on the ticket via email.

forum_topic_id.description: The ID of the associated community forum topic, if the ticket originated from a forum post.

problem_id.description: For incident tickets, the ID of the problem ticket this incident is linked to.

has_incidents.description: Whether this problem ticket has associated incident tickets.

is_public.description: Whether the ticket has any public-facing comments.

due_at.description: The due date for task-type tickets, in ISO 8601 format.

tags.description: Tags applied to the ticket for categorization and routing.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values set on the ticket.

satisfaction_rating.description: The satisfaction rating left by the customer, if any.

sharing_agreement_ids.description: IDs of sharing agreements used for the ticket.

custom_status_id.description: The ID of the custom ticket status, if custom statuses are enabled.

followup_ids.description: IDs of follow-up tickets created from this closed ticket.

ticket_form_id.description: The ID of the ticket form used for this ticket.

brand_id.description: The ID of the brand associated with this ticket.

allow_channelback.description: Whether channelback is enabled for this ticket.

allow_attachments.description: Whether the ticket allows file attachments.

from_messaging_channel.description: Whether the ticket originated from a messaging channel.

via.description: How the ticket was created (e.g., web, email, API, chat).

created_at.description: When the ticket was created, in ISO 8601 format.

updated_at.description: When the ticket was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

CustomField.description: A custom field key-value pair on the ticket.

id.description: The ID of the custom field.

value.description: The value of the custom field.

Via.description: Describes how the ticket was created or a comment was made.

channel.description: The channel through which the ticket was created (e.g., web, email, api, chat, voice, mobile, closed_ticket, any_channel).
